content: I completed my doctoral coarse and joined Kawai Lab. as a posdoc.
summary: I completed my doctoral coarse and joined Kawai Lab. as a posdoc.
title: joined Kawai Lab. as a posdoc.

content: Our new paper “Detecting Turbulent Breakdown for Predicting Transitional Boundary Layers in Wall-Modeled Large-Eddy Simulation” which I contributed to as a co-first author has appeared in AIAA Journal! We proposed a sensor that detects turbulent breakdown associated with the rapid increase in skin friction to activate the wall model. The proposed sensor-based wall-model calculation achieves high fidelity simulations of various scenarios of boundary layer transitions subject to wall heating/cooling with significantly reduced cost compared to the DNS!
summary: Our new paper “Detecting Turbulent Breakdown for Predicting Transitional Boundary Layers in Wall-Modeled Large-Eddy Simulation” which I contributed to as a co-first author has appeared in AIAA Journal! We proposed a sensor that detects turbulent breakdown associated with the rapid increase in skin friction to activate the wall model. The proposed sensor-based wall-model calculation achieves high fidelity simulations of various scenarios of boundary layer transitions subject to wall heating/cooling with significantly reduced cost compared to the DNS!
title: Our new paper 'Detecting Turbulent Breakdown for Predicting Transitional Boundary Layers in Wall-Modeled Large-Eddy Simulation' has appeared in AIAA Journal

content: A new Open Access paper “Compact representation of transonic airfoil buffet flows with observable-augmented machine learning” led by Associate Professor K. Fukami (Tohoku Univ., Japan) has appeared in Journal of Fluid Mechanics!
summary: A new Open Access paper “Compact representation of transonic airfoil buffet flows with observable-augmented machine learning” led by Associate Professor K. Fukami (Tohoku Univ., Japan) has appeared in Journal of Fluid Mechanics!
title: Our new paper 'Direct numerical simulation of subharmonic transition in pseudo-adiabatic, heated and cooled boundary layers' has appeared in International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flows

content: Our new Open Access paper “Direct numerical simulation of subharmonic transition in pseudo-adiabatic, heated, and cooled boundary layers” has appeared in International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flows! In the paper, it was found from the DNS data obtained using Fugaku supercomputer that, at a specific condition, wall cooling can alter the transition scenario from H-type to O-type by suppressing the first (TS) mode’s growth. Owing to the shift of transition scenario, the turbulent breakdown is delayed drastically. The [DNS database] presented in this paper will be available soon! (will be uploaded “here”)
summary: Our new Open Access paper “Direct numerical simulation of subharmonic transition in pseudo-adiabatic, heated, and cooled boundary layers” has appeared in International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flows! In the paper, it was found from the DNS data obtained using Fugaku supercomputer that, at a specific condition, wall cooling can alter the transition scenario from H-type to O-type by suppressing the first (TS) mode’s growth. Owing to the shift of transition scenario, the turbulent breakdown is delayed drastically. The [DNS database] presented in this paper will be available soon! (will be uploaded “here”)
title: Our new paper 'Direct numerical simulation of subharmonic transition in pseudo-adiabatic, heated and cooled boundary layers' has appeared in International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flows

content:
From downloading Hugo to deploying to github pages. Install hugo using homebrew.
brew install hugo Download hugo theme “PaperMod”.
hugo new site (myreponame).github.io cd (myreponame).github.io git init. # init git repo. Create config.yml
PaperMod recommends using yml rather than toml. Add this line to config.yml theme: "PaperMod" Create workflow file .github/workflows/gh-pages.yml (Ref)
Push local repo to remote repo at github
git remote add origin https://github.com/(MyGitHubAccount)/(RepoName).git git branch -M main git push -u origin main The homepage will be buit & deployed with GitHub Actions. The built static webpage files will appear in gh-pages branch.
Click setting tab and move to Pages, then change the branch from which the github pages is built (main to gh-pages).
title: Creating a webpage with Hugo & Publishing it with Github Pages.
